history: First drill on 2026-07-07 (pre-hardening) found three fail-open classes: future_timestamp and non_file_path passed when they should have blocked, and a config_boundary (max_age_days=0) rounding case. Commit 2b97a2c9 hardened the guard: future mtime is now treated as stale (reason=future_mtime), non-file paths block (reason=not_file), and max_age_days=0 on a same-day file is a documented pass boundary. This receipt re-runs the drill against the hardened guard.
regression_tests: tests/test_memory_freshness_guard.py (6 cases; each asserts ok is False plus specific reason, not assert-not-raised)
claim_ceiling: Proves the freshness guard blocked these mutation classes on 2026-07-07; not a general guarantee. The guard measures age only; the canonical policy (docs/structured-memory-freshness-policy.md) defines staleness as event-driven contradiction against PLAN, not age. That age-vs-event semantic gap is an owner decision and is out of scope for this rehearsal. config_boundary is a documented pass, not a proven-safe class.
